message:
fix(ensemble): set AllowAdvanced on bug-hunt and root-cause presets

The bug-hunt and root-cause-analysis presets reference advanced-tier
modes (clinical-operational, causal-inference, counterfactual, debiasing)
but did not set AllowAdvanced: true, causing validation failures on
spawn and dry-run.

Also closes beads: bd-49fb9, bd-1h4ox, bd-35bn4, bd-ughtn, bd-3hwc7,
bd-2t6v6 (ensemble features verified as implemented).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ghcp","title":"RCH: Implement rch binary detection and worker availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect RCH and check worker status.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.350046341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.349997629Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/tools/rch.go with RCHAdapter following the DCG adapter pattern.  {"id":"bd-1gkal","title":"Implement bulk prompt delivery","description":"Implement bulk prompt delivery.

## Functionality

Send assignment prompts to multiple panes efficiently.

## Default Prompt Template

```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Template Variables

- {bead_id}: The bead ID
- {bead_title}: Bead title/description
- {bead_type}: task, feature, bug, etc.
- {bead_deps}: Dependencies list
- {session}: Session name
- {pane}: Pane number

## Delivery Strategy

Option A: Sequential (safer)
- Send to pane 2, wait 1s
- Send to pane 3, wait 1s
- etc.

Option B: Parallel (faster)
- Send to all panes simultaneously
- May overwhelm if too many

Recommend: Sequential by default, --parallel flag for advanced users.

## Custom Templates

--prompt-template=<FILE> for custom prompts:
```
Custom marching orders here.
Bead: {bead_id}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.015184270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.752527055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.751110447Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gkal","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043619780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1h7g2","title":"Add --stagger flag for delayed agent launches","description":"Add --stagger SECONDS flag to control delay between agent launches.

## Purpose
Prevent overwhelming MCP Agent Mail when launching many agents at once.

## Syntax
```bash
ntm spawn myproject --cc=10 --cod=10 --stagger=30
```

## CRITICAL LESSONS LEARNED
1. Default stagger should be 30 seconds (not 20) - MCP Agent Mail gets overwhelmed
2. Wait 4 seconds after launching agent before sending marching orders
3. Send Enter TWICE after marching orders to ensure submission
4. Log confirmation that Enter was sent for debugging

## Behavior
- Default: 30 seconds (changed from 0 to protect MCP Agent Mail)
- When set: wait N seconds between each agent launch
- Progress indicator showing launch status with confirmation

## Implementation
- Add --stagger flag to spawn command (default: 30)
- Modify agent launch loop to include delay
- Show progress: [5/20] Launching agent... -> Sent message + Enter (x2)

## Code Pattern
```go
// After launching agent
time.Sleep(4 * time.Second)  // Wait for agent to initialize
sendKeys(pane, marchingOrders)
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // First Enter
time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // Second Enter - CRITICAL
log.Printf("  -> Sent message + Enter (x2)")
time.Sleep(staggerSeconds)
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ClaudeOpus","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:19:03.308328088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:38:52.053847706Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:38:52.053091161Z","close_reason":"Completed: Changed default stagger to 30s, added logging confirmation for Enter key delivery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

   - `ntm ensemble export <name> --output file.toml`

- Reject remote URLs by default
- Require SHA256 checksum for remote files
- Sanitize file paths and disallow traversal

- Log validation failures + checksum results
- Log import source + installed preset id

## Tests

- Integration: export→import roundtrip
- E2E: local import succeeds; remote import rejected unless allowed

 {"id":"bd-2t1j3","title":"Unit Tests: AccountRotator caam integration","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for AccountRotator covering caam CLI integration, account switching, and provider normalization.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/account_rotator_test.go
package swarm

func TestAccountRotator_SwitchAccount(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name           string
        provider       string
        mockCaamOutput string
        mockCaamError  error
        expectError    bool
    }{
        {
            name:     "switch_anthropic_success",
            provider: "anthropic",
            mockCaamOutput: `{"switched": true, "from": "claude_main", "to": "claude_backup"}`,
        },
        {
            name:     "switch_openai_success",
            provider: "openai",
            mockCaamOutput: `{"switched": true, "from": "gpt_main", "to": "gpt_backup"}`,
        },
        {
            name:        "switch_no_accounts",
            provider:    "google",
            mockCaamError: fmt.Errorf("no alternate accounts configured"),
            expectError: true,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            mockCmd := &MockCommandRunner{
                output: tt.mockCaamOutput,
                err:    tt.mockCaamError,
            }
            
            rotator := NewAccountRotator(mockCmd)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Switching account for provider: %s", tt.provider)
            
            record, err := rotator.SwitchAccount(tt.provider)
            
            if tt.expectError {
                assert.Error(t, err)
                t.Logf("[TEST] Expected error: %v", err)
            } else {
                require.NoError(t, err)
                t.Logf("[TEST] Switched from %s to %s", record.FromAccount, record.ToAccount)
                assert.NotEmpty(t, record.ToAccount)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestAccountRotator_ProviderNormalization(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input    string
        expected string
    }{
        {"cc", "anthropic"},
        {"claude", "anthropic"},
        {"cod", "openai"},
        {"codex", "openai"},
        {"gmi", "google"},
        {"gemini", "google"},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.input, func(t *testing.T) {
            result := normalizeProvider(tt.input)
            t.Logf("[TEST] %s -> %s", tt.input, result)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}

func TestAccountRotator_DetectCurrentAccount(t *testing.T) {
    mockCmd := &MockCommandRunner{
        output: `{"current": "claude_backup", "provider": "anthropic"}`,
    }
    
    rotator := NewAccountRotator(mockCmd)
    
    account, err := rotator.DetectCurrentAccount("anthropic")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Current account: %s", account)
    assert.Equal(t, "claude_backup", account)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test account switching for all 3 providers
- [ ] Test error handling when no accounts available
- [ ] Test provider name normalization
- [ ] Test current account detection
- [ ] Mock caam command execution
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:32:26.665479949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:52:16.992942095Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:52:16.992850903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t1j3","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:32:26.682470023Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2t6v6","title":"Feature: Ensemble Dry Run Mode - Preview spawn without executing","description":"# Feature: Ensemble Dry Run Mode

## Problem Statement
Users hesitate to run ensembles because they can't preview what will happen. They worry about:
- Wasting tokens on wrong ensemble
- Not understanding mode selection
- Unclear budget allocation

## Solution
Add `--dry-run` flag to ensemble spawn command that shows exactly what would happen without creating sessions or writing state.

## User Experience
```bash
ntm ensemble spawn project-diagnosis --dry-run
# Output:
# DRY RUN - No sessions will be created
#
# Ensemble: project-diagnosis
# Question: (interactive prompt or --question flag)
#
# Modes (5):
#   A3  systems-thinking     [CORE]   ~800 tokens
#   D2  adversarial-review   [CORE]   ~750 tokens
#   E1  dependency-mapping   [CORE]   ~600 tokens
#   G2  failure-mode         [CORE]   ~700 tokens
#   L1  perspective-taking   [CORE]   ~650 tokens
#
# Agent Distribution:
#   Claude Code: 3 agents
#   Codex: 2 agents
#
# Budget Allocation:
#   Per-agent cap: 2000 tokens
#   Total cap: 10000 tokens
#   Estimated usage: ~3500 tokens
#
# Preamble Preview (first mode):
#   [First 200 chars of systems-thinking preamble...]
#
# To execute: Remove --dry-run flag
```

## Robot API
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION --dry-run --question='...'
# Returns JSON with all the above structured
```

## Implementation
- Add --dry-run flag to ensemble spawn command
- Reuse existing spawn logic up to tmux creation (no side effects)
- Deterministic ordering for mode list + agents
- Preamble preview length configurable (default 200 chars)
- Token estimates use TokenEstimator (no guesswork)

## Logging
- Log dry-run invocation with session, ensemble id, mode count
- Log estimated tokens + budget cap

## Tests
- Unit: dry-run plan generation does not call tmux client
- Unit: JSON output schema + deterministic ordering
- E2E: dry-run creates no sessions and no state files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --dry-run shows modes, agents, budget without spawning
- [ ] Preamble preview shows first N chars (configurable)
- [ ] Token estimates calculated from preamble lengths
- [ ] Robot API returns structured JSON
- [ ] No tmux sessions created in dry-run
- [ ] No state persisted in dry-run","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:02:17.200488184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:21:53.416856015Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t6v6","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:02:17.221499717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t6v6","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:21:06.289540560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t6v6","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:21:22.609741695Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t6v6","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:21:53.416831209Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t6v6","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:21:38.173509925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

#

ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION --dry-run --question='...'

## Problem Statement
Users face choice paralysis with 9 ensemble presets. They ask:
- Which ensemble is best for my question?
- What's the difference between bug-hunt and root-cause?
- Am I picking the right one?

## Solution
Analyze question text and recommend the best-fit ensemble with explanation (deterministic, rule-based).

## User Experience
```bash
ntm ensemble suggest "What security vulnerabilities exist in the auth system?"
# Output:
# Recommended: safety-risk
# Confidence: HIGH
#
# Reason: matched keywords: security, vulnerabilities → safety-risk
#
# Alternatives:
#   bug-hunt (0.65) - If looking for specific implementation bugs
#   root-cause (0.45) - If investigating known security incident
#
# To run: ntm ensemble spawn safety-risk --question "..."
```

## Piping Support
```bash
# Get just the ID for piping
ntm ensemble suggest --question "What security..." --id-only
# Output: safety-risk

# Pipe to spawn
ntm ensemble spawn $(ntm ensemble suggest --question "..." --id-only) --question "..."
```

## Matching Algorithm (Deterministic)
Pattern-based (no ML) for reliability:

```go
var ensembleKeywords = map[string][]string{
    "project-diagnosis":   {"architecture", "structure", "codebase", "overview", "health"},
    "idea-forge":          {"feature", "improve", "add", "enhance", "brainstorm", "ideas"},
    "spec-critique":       {"spec", "requirements", "api", "interface", "contract"},
    "safety-risk":         {"security", "vulnerability", "threat", "compliance", "risk"},
    "architecture-review": {"design", "pattern", "refactor", "structure", "scalable"},
    "tech-debt-triage":    {"debt", "legacy", "cleanup", "deprecat", "upgrade"},
    "bug-hunt":            {"bug", "error", "fail", "broken", "issue"},
    "root-cause":          {"why", "cause", "investigate", "debug", "trace"},
    "strategic-planning":  {"roadmap", "priority", "plan", "strategy", "quarter"},
}
```

Scoring rules:
- Lowercase + simple stemming (suffix trim)
- Keyword count × weights
- Tie-breaker: preset priority order
- Confidence = normalized score → HIGH/MED/LOW

## Robot API
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-suggest --question="..."
# Returns: {ensemble_id, confidence, reason, alternatives: [...]}
```

## Logging
- Log matched keywords + scores
- Log chosen preset + confidence

## Tests

- Unit: id-only output
- E2E: suggest + spawn piping
- E2E: robot JSON schema + deterministic ordering

- [ ] Returns top match with confidence score
- [ ] Shows 2-3 alternatives with scores
- [ ] Explains why each was matched/ranked
- [ ] --id-only flag for piping

 {"id":"bd-35chh","title":"Test: Unit tests for BeadScanner","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AllocationCalculator tier calculations, ensuring correct agent allocation based on bead counts.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/allocation_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestCalculateTier(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        beadCount int
        wantTier  int
    }{
        // Tier 1: 400+ beads
        {"tier1_minimum", 400, 1},
        {"tier1_high", 1000, 1},
        {"tier1_exact_boundary", 400, 1},
        
        // Tier 2: 100-399 beads
        {"tier2_minimum", 100, 2},
        {"tier2_maximum", 399, 2},
        {"tier2_middle", 250, 2},
        
        // Tier 3: <100 beads
        {"tier3_maximum", 99, 3},
        {"tier3_minimum", 1, 3},
        {"tier3_zero", 0, 3},
        {"tier3_negative", -1, 3}, // Edge case
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Testing bead count %d, expecting tier %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier)
            got := calc.CalculateTier(tt.beadCount)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTier, got, 
                "beadCount=%d should be tier %d, got %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier, got)
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateProjectAllocation(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
        Tier1Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 3, Cod: 2, Gmi: 1},
        Tier2Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 2, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        Tier3Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 0},
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name       string
        project    ProjectBeadCount
        wantCC     int
        wantCod    int
        wantGmi    int
        wantTotal  int
    }{
        {
            name:      "tier1_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
            wantCC:    3, wantCod: 2, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 6,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier2_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
            wantCC:    2, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 4,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier3_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
            wantCC:    1, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 0, wantTotal: 2,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Project: %s, Beads: %d", tt.project.Name, tt.project.OpenBeads)
            
            alloc := calc.CalculateProjectAllocation(tt.project)

 {"id":"bd-3hvz5","title":"AutoRespawner: Re-inject marching orders after respawn","description":"## AutoRespawner: Re-inject Marching Orders After Respawn

### Purpose
Implement post-respawn prompt injection to get the fresh agent working immediately.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Post-Respawn Injection:**
```go
// fullRespawnCycle performs complete respawn including prompt injection.
func (r *AutoRespawner) fullRespawnCycle(ctx context.Context, event LimitEvent, projectPath string) (*RespawnResult, error) {
    start := time.Now()
    result := &RespawnResult{
        SessionPane: event.SessionPane,
        AgentType:   event.AgentType,
        RespawnedAt: start,
    }
    
    // 1. Kill the agent
    if err := r.killWithFallback(ctx, event.SessionPane, event.AgentType); err != nil {
        result.Error = fmt.Sprintf("kill failed: %v", err)
        return result, err
    }
    
    // 2. Optionally rotate account
    if r.AccountRotator != nil && r.Config.AutoRotateAccounts {
        provider := agentToProvider(event.AgentType)
        record, err := r.AccountRotator.SwitchAccount(provider)
        if err != nil {
            log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] account_rotation_failed", "error", err)
        } else {
            result.AccountRotated = true
            result.PreviousAccount = record.FromAccount
            result.NewAccount = record.ToAccount
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Respawn agent
    if err := r.respawnAgent(event.SessionPane, event.AgentType, projectPath); err != nil {
        result.Error = fmt.Sprintf("respawn failed: %v", err)
        return result, err
    }
    
    // 4. Re-inject marching orders
    prompt := r.getMarchingOrders(event.AgentType)
    injResult, err := r.PromptInjector.InjectPrompt(event.SessionPane, event.AgentType, prompt)
    if err != nil {
        log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] prompt_injection_failed", "error", err)
        // Don't fail the whole respawn for prompt injection failure
    } else {
        log.Info("[AutoRespawner] marching_orders_injected", 
            "session_pane", event.SessionPane, 
            "duration", injResult.Duration)
    }
    
    result.Success = true
    result.Duration = time.Since(start)
    return result, nil
}

// getMarchingOrders returns the appropriate marching orders for the agent type.
func (r *AutoRespawner) getMarchingOrders(agentType string) string {
    // Use agent-specific template if available
    if r.Config.MarchingOrders != nil {
        if tmpl, ok := r.Config.MarchingOrders[agentType]; ok && tmpl != "" {
            return tmpl
        }
        if r.Config.MarchingOrders["default"] != "" {
            return r.Config.MarchingOrders["default"]
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to hardcoded default
    return defaultMarchingOrders
}

const defaultMarchingOrders = `First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file super carefully and understand ALL of it!
Then use bv --robot-triage to find the most important work to focus on.
Then claim a bead with br update <id> --status in_progress and get to work!`
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log full cycle start: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_cycle_start session_pane=%s`
- Log account rotation: `[AutoRespawner] account_rotated from=%s to=%s`
- Log prompt injection: `[AutoRespawner] marching_orders_injected session_pane=%s`
- Log cycle complete: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_cycle_complete session_pane=%s success=%t duration=%v`

- [ ] Agent-specific marching orders templates
- [ ] Integration with PromptInjector
- [ ] Non-fatal prompt injection failures

 {"id":"bd-3hxdl","title":"Task: Add last_output_ts field to pane status","description":"## What

Add `last_output_ts` field tracking when the pane last produced output.

## Why

Timeout-based stuck detection needs to know when output last changed.
"No output for 5 minutes" is a strong signal the agent is stuck.
Currently this requires external state tracking.

## Implementation

```go
type PaneOutputTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    lastOutput  map[int]time.Time  // pane_index -> last output time
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Update(paneIndex int) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastOutput[paneIndex] = time.Now()
}

func (t *PaneOutputTracker) Get(paneIndex int) time.Time {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    return t.lastOutput[paneIndex]
}
```

## State Persistence

- Option A: In-memory only (reset on ntm restart) - simpler
- Option B: Persist to session metadata file - survives restarts

Recommend Option A for MVP, add persistence later if needed.

## How to Detect Output Changes

During status gathering, compare current pane content hash with previous.
If changed, update timestamp.

```go
func checkOutputChanged(pane int) bool {
    current := capturePaneContent(pane)
    hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(current))
    if hash != lastHash[pane] {
        lastHash[pane] = hash
        return true
    }
    return false
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",  // ISO 8601
    "seconds_since_output": 312                 // Convenience field
  }]
}
```

 {"id":"bd-t9qp4","title":"Feature: Robot Bulk Assign Command (--robot-bulk-assign)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-bulk-assign <session>` command that intelligently allocates
beads to agent panes in a single operation based on bv triage output.

## Background

Current bead allocation workflow is tedious:
1. Run `bv --robot-triage` to get prioritized beads
2. Parse the recommendations manually
3. For each pane, decide which bead to assign
4. Construct and send individual prompts to each pane
5. Track the allocation mentally

This should be one command that does it all.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
# Auto-fetch bv triage and allocate
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv

# Provide explicit allocation
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --allocation='{"2":"bd-92e1","3":"bd-55pm"}'

# Dry run - show plan without executing
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv --dry-run
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "strategy": "impact",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:16:00Z",
  "assignments": [
    {
      "pane": 2,
      "bead": "bd-92e1",
      "bead_title": "Add assignment tracking",
      "reason": "highest_unblocks (9 items)",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true
    },
    {
      "pane": 3,
      "bead": "bd-55pm",
      "bead_title": "Direct bv integration",
      "reason": "high_unblocks (7 items)",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true
    },
    // ... more assignments
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 15,
    "assigned": 15,
    "skipped": 0,
    "failed": 0
  },
  "unassigned_beads": ["bd-xyz"],  // More beads than panes
  "unassigned_panes": []           // More panes than beads (if any)
}
```

## Allocation Strategies

When using `--from-bv`, use these strategies:

| Strategy | Logic |
|----------|-------|
| impact (default) | Highest `unblocks_count` first |
| ready | Only `ready` status beads, by priority |
| stale | `in_progress` with no recent activity first |
| balanced | Mix of blockers, ready, and stale |

## Flags

- `--from-bv`: Fetch bv triage and auto-allocate
- `--strategy=<S>`: Allocation strategy (impact, ready, stale, balanced)
- `--allocation=<JSON>`: Explicit pane-to-bead mapping
- `--dry-run`: Show plan without executing
- `--skip-panes=<N,N>`: Skip specific panes (e.g., leave them on current work)
- `--prompt-template=<FILE>`: Custom prompt template for assignments

## Integration with BV

E2E test of round-robin strategy via `ntm assign --strategy=round-robin` across mixed agent types.

- Claude 1: beads 1, 6
- Claude 2: beads 2, 7
- Codex 1: beads 3, 8
- Codex 2: beads 4, 9
- Gemini 1: beads 5, 10

- Each agent gets exactly 2 beads
- Beads assigned in order
- No duplicates

    echo "[E2E-ROUNDROBIN] $(date -Iseconds) test=$1 step=$2 status=$3 details=\"$4\""

 {"id":"bd-uzasi","title":"Task: Implement mode output caching system","description":"## Implementation
Add caching layer for mode outputs (prefer existing internal/ensemble files; add new file only if needed):

1. CacheManager:
   - Disk-backed store under `.ntm/ensemble-cache/`
   - `CacheKey = ContextHash + ModeID + ModeVersion + ConfigHash`
   - Methods: Get/Put/Invalidate/Stats

2. Invalidation:
   - Context hash change
   - Mode version bump
   - TTL expiry (default 24h, configurable)
   - `--no-cache` explicit bypass

3. CLI integration:
   - `--use-cache/--no-cache`
   - `ntm ensemble cache stats` and `cache clear`

## Logging
- Log hit/miss + invalidation reason per mode

## Tests
- Unit: key derivation + TTL expiry
- Integration: cache persists across runs
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh
